label: Governance & Policy
desc: International institutions, treaties, and policy data from primary governmental sources.

sources.name: United Nations
sources.desc: Global intergovernmental forum; resolutions, treaties, and peacekeeping data.

sources.name: World Bank Open Data
sources.desc: Free access to global development data — GDP, poverty, education indicators.

sources.name: OECD iLibrary
sources.desc: Economic and social policy research from 38 member nations.

sources.name: EU Open Data Portal
sources.desc: Official datasets from all European Union institutions.

sources.name: U.S. Data.gov
sources.desc: U.S. federal government's open data catalog — 250,000+ datasets.

label: Science & Research
desc: Peer-reviewed research, space exploration data, and climate science from leading institutions.

sources.name: NASA Open Data
sources.desc: Space exploration datasets, Earth observation imagery, and mission archives.

sources.name: WHO Global Health Observatory
sources.desc: Global health statistics, disease surveillance, and mortality data.

sources.name: NOAA Climate Data
sources.desc: Sea level, temperature, hurricanes, and climate trends from NOAA.

sources.name: PubMed / NCBI
sources.desc: 35M+ biomedical research citations and full-text articles.

sources.name: arXiv
sources.desc: Open preprint server for physics, math, CS, biology, economics, and more.

sources.name: CERN Open Data
sources.desc: Particle collision datasets from ATLAS and CMS experiments at CERN.

label: Environment & Climate
desc: Authoritative environmental monitoring, climate modeling, and conservation data.

sources.name: IPCC Reports
sources.desc: Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change — synthesis and assessment reports.

sources.name: UNEP Live
sources.desc: UN Environment Programme — ecosystem data, pollution, and biodiversity.

sources.name: Global Forest Watch
sources.desc: Near-real-time forest monitoring using satellite imagery.

sources.name: NASA Earth Observatory
sources.desc: Satellite imagery and scientific analysis of Earth's land, ocean, and atmosphere.

sources.name: EPA Environmental Data
sources.desc: Air quality, water, toxics, and environmental justice data from the U.S. EPA.

sources.name: Copernicus Climate Change Service
sources.desc: EU Copernicus Programme: ERA5 reanalysis, sea ice, and temperature records.

label: Arts & Culture
desc: Cultural heritage, arts, archives, and humanities from the world's leading institutions.

sources.name: UNESCO Culture
sources.desc: Intangible cultural heritage, world heritage sites, and cultural diversity data.

sources.name: Library of Congress
sources.desc: America's national library — 170M+ items, free digital collections.

sources.name: Europeana
sources.desc: 55M+ digitized works from 3,000+ European cultural institutions.

sources.name: Internet Archive
sources.desc: Wayback Machine, books, audio, video — preserving the digital and analog record.

sources.name: Smithsonian Open Access
sources.desc: 4.4M+ digitized cultural artifacts and scientific specimens, CC0 licensed.

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons
sources.desc: 87M+ freely usable media files — images, audio, video, and documents.

label: Economy & Finance
desc: Macroeconomic indicators, financial stability reports, and trade statistics from primary sources.

sources.name: World Bank Data
sources.desc: 1,600+ development indicators for 217 economies from 1960 to present.

sources.name: IMF Data
sources.desc: World Economic Outlook, International Financial Statistics, and balance of payments data.

sources.name: OECD Statistics
sources.desc: National accounts, employment, trade, and productivity for OECD members.

sources.name: Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED)
sources.desc: 800,000+ U.S. and international economic time series from the St. Louis Fed.

sources.name: Eurostat
sources.desc: Official statistics for the European Union — 27 member states.

sources.name: UN Comtrade
sources.desc: Global merchandise trade statistics — imports and exports by country and commodity.

label: Global Health
desc: Disease surveillance, health systems data, and population health metrics from global health authorities.

sources.name: WHO Data
sources.desc: Global Health Observatory, mortality data, and NCD surveillance from the World Health Organization.

sources.name: CDC Data & Statistics
sources.desc: U.S. disease surveillance, chronic conditions, and public health data.

sources.name: NIH Data Sharing
sources.desc: Research datasets from the National Institutes of Health.

sources.name: Our World in Data — Health
sources.desc: Long-run health metrics with source data from WHO, IHME, UNICEF.

sources.name: IHME Global Burden of Disease
sources.desc: Comprehensive study of mortality and disability from 350+ diseases and injuries.

sources.name: UNAIDS Data
sources.desc: HIV/AIDS epidemiology, treatment, and program data from UNAIDS.

label: Education & Knowledge
desc: Open educational resources, academic knowledge, and global literacy data.

sources.name: MIT OpenCourseWare
sources.desc: Free course materials from nearly all MIT courses — 2,500+ courses online.

sources.name: Khan Academy
sources.desc: Free world-class education for anyone, anywhere — K-12 through college.

sources.name: Wikipedia
sources.desc: The world's largest free encyclopedia — 60M+ articles in 300+ languages.

sources.name: Project Gutenberg
sources.desc: 70,000+ free e-books — public domain literary works.

sources.name: UNESCO Institute for Statistics
sources.desc: Global literacy rates, enrollment ratios, and education attainment data.

sources.name: JSTOR Open Access
sources.desc: Millions of academic articles available free — no login required for open content.

sources.desc: Investigative reports on abuses, conflict, discrimination, and accountability.

sources.name: Freedom House
sources.desc: Annual Freedom in the World and Freedom on the Net country assessments.
